data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child (4-bucket: FULLY):
- Parent AC1 ("A shared, single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema exists; both producer output and consumer reads validate against it") → 8937 AC1 (_snapshot_schema.py TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT) + AC2 (fetch_snapshot validated vs schema; FAILS on pre-fix conditions — issuelinks absent / comment under wrong key). FULLY.
- Parent AC2 ("One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") → 8937 AC3 (round-trip fetcher→differs→applier through production path; fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read). Real-payload fixtures correctly delegated to fe57 (depends_on declared). FULLY.
- Parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → directly satisfied by the joint round-trip contract; reinforced by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). FULLY.
- Contributes to SC5 / AC6-posture via 8937 AC4 (semantic assertions; no whole-blob golden equality / no mock-call-count) and to AC7 (suite green).

DELEGATION — criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT items (fixtures → fe57; applier collapse → 33d0; telemetry → 2359) are consistent with the roster: AC3/SC2 → 33d0; AC4/SC3 → 2359; AC5/SC4 → 822a; fixtures/fake foundation → fe57; verified-fake honesty → 59ef. AC7 is a structural/aggregate epic roll-up. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 8937 itself for its owned criteria.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC1/AC2/SC1 FULLY (this child); SC5/AC7 contributed; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence — see below).

ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT a coverage gap of 8937's owned criteria): Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including 8937 (AC4), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing artifact for FUTURE/new reconciler tests. 8937 (the keystone) is repeatedly named across the roster as its natural home. This identical structured finding is ALREADY filed on this ticket (prior G3 audits) and across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57; it is NOT re-filed here to avoid duplication. Suggested fix remains: add a new AC to 8937 ("a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child.

VERDICT for this pairing: child 8937's owned criteria (AC1/AC2/SC1) are FULLY covered; no NEW coverage finding is attributable to this child. The only roster-level gap (AC6) is already tracked. Clean pairing.
